title: Externalities: Foundational concepts
description: Practice what you have learned about identifying externalities and using graphs to identify both market equilibrium and socially optimal outcomes in this exercise.
topic_path: Market failure and the role of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes where total surplus is maximized when an externality exists in a market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The quantity where marginal social cost equals marginal social benefit.
The quantity where marginal social cost equals marginal private benefit.
The quantity where marginal social benefit equals marginal private cost.
The quantity where marginal social cost is higher than marginal private cost
The quantity where marginal social benefit is higher than marginal private benefit

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations best describes a positive externality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You bake a pie that makes the whole neighborhood smell delicious.
You eat chocolate until you don’t like chocolate anymore.
One firm enters a market and steals market share from another firm.
You have dinner with a friend and split the bill.
You make a smelly sandwich that makes your neighbor feel sick.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations best describes a negative externality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You make a sardine and limburger sandwich that makes your dorm smell terrible.
You drink coffee until you feel ill.
One firm's invention opens new doors for invention by other firms.
You face all of the consequences of an action.
You bake your neighbor a cake.

assessment_items.question_content:
When snods are produced, one by-product is green toxic sludge. If the market is not regulated, snod producers dump the sludge in a nearby river, which would need cleaning if the water were to be used for household use or recreation.

**What is being described, and how does the quantity produced in the market compare to the socially optimal quantity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A negative externality; the market quantity is higher than the socially optimal quantity
A negative externality; the market quantity is equal to the socially optimal quantity
A public good; the market quantity is lower than the socially optimal quantity
A positive externality; the market quantity is higher than the socially optimal quantity
A positive externality; the market quantity is lower than the socially optimal quantity

assessment_items.question_content:
The consumption of good Z creates positive externalities, but there are no externalities in producing good Z.

**What must be true at the market equilibrium for good Z if the market is not regulated?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Marginal social benefit is greater than marginal social cost
Marginal social benefit equals marginal social cost
Marginal private benefit is greater than marginal social benefit
Marginal social cost is greater than marginal private cost
Marginal social cost is greater than marginal private benefit

assessment_items.question_content:
The production of good B creates negative externalities, but there are no externalities in the consumption of good B.

**What must be true at the market equilibrium for good B if the market is not regulated?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Marginal social benefit is less than marginal social cost
Marginal social cost equals marginal social benefit.
Marginal private cost is greater than marginal social cost
Marginal social benefit is greater than marginal private benefit
Marginal social cost is less than marginal private benefit

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a positive externality exists in the consumption of chive butter, which of the following best describes this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The marginal social benefit exceeds marginal private benefit.
The marginal social cost of production exceeds the marginal private cost of production
The marginal social cost of production equals the marginal private cost of production
The marginal social benefit is less than the marginal private benefit.
The marginal social cost of production is less than the marginal private cost of production

assessment_items.question_content:
There is a negative production externality in the market for peanut butter. 

**Which of the following best describes what is true about quantity sold in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The marginal social cost of that quantity exceeds its marginal private cost.
The marginal social cost of benefit exceeds the marginal private benefit of consumption.
The marginal social cost of production equals the marginal private cost of production
The marginal social cost is less than the marginal private cost.
The marginal social benefit of consumption is less than the marginal private benefit of consumption

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the market for flazzles, including the marginal social benefit (MSB), marginal social cost (MSC), and marginal private benefit (MPB).

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a9c9d15e811d34fa2020341d327969b8c3d38012)


**What is the socially optimal quantity, and what is the quantity that exists in equilibrium without regulation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Socially optimal: $Q=28$;  Without regulation: $Q=20$
Socially optimal: $Q=20$; Without regulation: $Q=36$
Socially optimal: $Q=28$; Without regulation: $Q=28$
Socially optimal: $Q=28$; Without regulation: $Q=12$
Socially optimal: $Q=20$; Without regulation: $Q=12$

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private benefit (MPB), and the marginal social benefit) associated with the market for snizzles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24b2b288c3cfd0846212ae9291e125fdd4eda276)

**What is the socially optimal quantity and what price would people be willing to pay for that quantity if the externality shown here is internalized?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Socially optimal quantity=$10$; Willing to pay $\$14$
Socially optimal quantity=$8$; Willing to pay $\$16$
Socially optimal quantity=$10$; Willing to pay $\$10$
Socially optimal quantity=$8$; Willing to pay $\$12$
Socially optimal quantity=$10$; Willing to pay $\$12$

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private benefit (MPB), and the marginal social benefit) associated with the market for snizzles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24b2b288c3cfd0846212ae9291e125fdd4eda276)

**What is the marginal social benefit of the last unit purchased in this market with no intervention?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

This graph shows the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private benefit (MPB), and the marginal social benefit) associated with the market for snizzles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24b2b288c3cfd0846212ae9291e125fdd4eda276)

**How much more will be sold in this market if people internalize this externality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $2$ units
$4$ units
$10$ units
$8$ units

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private benefit (MPB), and the marginal social benefit (MSB) associated with the market for snizzles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24b2b288c3cfd0846212ae9291e125fdd4eda276)

**What kind of externality is shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A positive externality in consumption
A negative externality in consumption
A positive externality in production
A negative externality in production
A positive externality in production and a negative externality in consumption.

assessment_items.question_content:
The market for gleems is shown in this graph, including marginal social cost (MSC), marginal social benefit (MSB), and marginal private cost (MPC). 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ba5a4d49e119030c1b2cee62758adabae7ecb1b5)

**Which of the following is true based on this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There are negative externalities in the production of gleems and the market will produce more than is socially optimal.
There are positive externalities in the consumption of gleems and the market will produce less than is socially optimal.
Consumers of gleems should be subsidized to achieve the socially optimal quantity of gleems.
This market produces the socially optimal quantity of gleems without intervention.
The market quantity has a marginal social benefit that exceeds the marginal social cost.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private cost (MPC), and marginal social benefit for graggles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8868e1ef337f4166193d1662a5d064ba93f232cc)

**What is true about the market shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There are negative externalities associated with producing graggles.
There are negative externalities associated with consuming graggles.
There are positive externalities associated with consuming graggles.
There are positive externalities associated with producing graggles.
The external costs of producing graggles are decreasing at a decreasing rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the marginal social cost (MSC), marginal private cost (MPC), and marginal social benefit for graggles.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8868e1ef337f4166193d1662a5d064ba93f232cc)

**What is the market quantity and price, and what is the socially optimal quantity and price?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content:
$\begin{align} \text{Market}: & Q_b,P_y\\\\
\text{Socially optimal: } & Q_a,P_x\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Market}: & Q_a,P_x\\\\
\text{Socially optimal: } & Q_b,P_w\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Market}: & Q_a,P_x\\\\
\text{Socially optimal: } & Q_a,P_z\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Market:} & Q_b,P_y\\\\
\text{Socially optimal: } & \text{none}\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Market}: & Q_b,P_w\\\\
\text{Socially optimal: } & Q_a,P_w\end{align}$
